name: cento
summary: Unified cento CLI facade for built-ins, tool dispatch, alias dispatch, shell integration, and terminal help browsing.
notes: Registered tools can be invoked directly as `cento TOOL [args...]`.
Configured aliases can be invoked directly as `cento ALIAS [args...]`.
Use `cento docs` or `cento interactive` when you need the built-in command surface explained from the canonical JSON source.
`cento install terminal` installs the managed Zsh/Oh My Zsh completion init plus the Cento prompt segment.

routing.name: tool-routing
routing.summary: Run a registered tool directly by id, including `cento factory` for plan, dispatch, and Safe Integrator artifacts.

routing.name: alias-routing
routing.summary: Run a configured alias directly from `~/.config/cento/aliases.sh`.

flags.name: -h, --help
flags.summary: Show root CLI help without naming the `help` subcommand.

commands.name: interactive
commands.summary: Open the Bubble Tea TUI for built-ins, tools, aliases, and docs.

commands.name: docs
commands.summary: Print cento CLI docs from the canonical JSON source.

flags.name: --path
flags.summary: Print the path to the canonical JSON document.

commands.name: tools
commands.summary: List registered cento tools from the tool registry.

commands.name: conf
commands.summary: Open or print the cento alias config file.

flags.name: --path
flags.summary: Print the config path instead of opening it in an editor.

commands.name: completion
commands.summary: Print shell completion for a supported shell.

commands.name: install
commands.summary: Install cento shell and terminal integration.
details: `zsh` installs completion under `~/.config/cento/completions/_cento`, writes `~/.config/cento/init.zsh`, injects one guarded source block into `~/.zshrc`, and adds a right-prompt segment like `[cento:linux:host]`.
`tmux` writes `~/.config/cento/tmux.conf`, injects one guarded source block into `~/.tmux.conf`, and reloads tmux when a server is running.
`terminal` and `all` install the Zsh/Oh My Zsh prompt and completion path only. Run `cento install tmux` explicitly if you want tmux status integration.

commands.name: tmux
commands.summary: Manage the cento tmux status badge integration.
details: `cento tmux badge` prints the short label rendered in tmux status-left.
Set `CENTO_TMUX_BADGE` to override the label and `CENTO_TMUX_BADGE_HOST=1` to append the short hostname.
The generated fragment preserves the current status-left as `@cento_status_left_base` and prepends the Cento badge. Tmux integration is opt-in via `cento install tmux`.

commands.name: run
commands.summary: Run a registered tool by id.

commands.name: factory
commands.summary: Registered tool route for the no-model Cento Factory planning, dispatch dry-run, and Safe Integrator workflow.
examples: cento factory intake "develop me a career consulting module" --dry-run --out workspace/runs/factory/factory-planning-e2e
cento factory runtime prepare factory-runtime-adapters-e2e --task factory-runtime-task-01 --runtime noop --dry-run
cento factory runtime launch factory-runtime-adapters-e2e --task factory-runtime-task-01 --runtime noop --dry-run
cento factory runtime status factory-runtime-adapters-e2e --task factory-runtime-task-01 --json
cento factory runtime collect factory-runtime-adapters-e2e --task factory-runtime-task-01
cento factory runtime cancel factory-runtime-adapters-e2e --task factory-runtime-task-01 --dry-run
details: This command is routed through data/tools.json and scripts/factory.py.

Factory defaults to deterministic no-model planning, queueing, lease simulation, dry-run dispatch, patch collection, Safe Integrator branch/apply/validate gates, rollback metadata, merge readiness, release evidence, Autopilot dry-run control cycles, and runtime adapter contracts. Live Taskstream creation requires --apply, while integration Taskstream sync is a dry-run preview by default.

commands.name: storage
commands.summary: Registered tool route for the no-delete Cento artifact catalog and retention planner.
details: This command is routed through data/tools.json and scripts/storage.py.

Storage v1 never deletes artifacts and never uploads to cloud. It catalogs, hashes, classifies, plans lifecycle actions, verifies hashes, and renders operator reports so Factory and Autopilot can scale without artifact chaos.
